verbGlossary.CLASSIFIED UNDER: {source} is categorized using {target} taxonomy (e.g., safety observation tagged to an OSHA hazard category, punch item mapped to a MasterFormat division)
verbGlossary.CITED FOR: {source} formally references a specific violation of {target} (e.g., safety violation citing an OSHA hazard rule, deficiency notice citing ADA non-compliance)
verbGlossary.LOGGED PER: {source} is documented following {target} recordkeeping requirements (e.g., incident record logged per OSHA 300 log format)
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTED PER: Supporting record is captured in the format required by {target} (e.g., witness statement documented per OSHA recordkeeping requirements)
verbGlossary.CODED BY: {source} is assigned a classification code from {target} (e.g., incident coded by ANSI injury type, observation coded by standard cost code)
verbGlossary.GOVERNED BY: {source} or review is required and regulated by {target} or standard (e.g., building inspection governed by IBC requirements)
verbGlossary.MEASURED AGAINST: {source} pass/fail or conformance outcome is evaluated relative to {target} acceptance criteria (e.g., inspection item measured against IBC tolerances)
verbGlossary.REQUIRED BY: Scheduled activity exists because {target} mandates it at specific construction milestones (e.g., concrete pour inspection required by IBC)
verbGlossary.MANDATED BY: {source}inspection is legally required by {target} code (e.g., structural steel inspection mandated by IBC Section 1705)
verbGlossary.TESTED PER: Test report or inspection item follows {target} prescribed testing protocol (e.g., fire suppression system tested per NFPA 25, material test per specification section)
verbGlossary.VERIFIED AGAINST: {source} confirms compliance with {target} through independent verification (e.g., commissioning test verified against net-zero energy performance targets)
verbGlossary.VIOLATES: {source} documents a failure to meet {target} requirements (e.g., NCR issued for specification non-conformance, safety observation citing NFPA fire code violation)
verbGlossary.CONFLICTS WITH: {source}issue identifies a contradiction with {target} (e.g., MEP routing clashes with structural specification requirements)
verbGlossary.TAGGED TO: {source} is linked to {target} for traceability (e.g., material test report tagged to a UniFormat element, coordination issue tagged to an IFC class)
verbGlossary.CHARGED TO: {source}cost is allocated to a WBS line or cost code in {target} (e.g., punch item resolution cost charged to standard cost code)
verbGlossary.ALLOCATED AGAINST: {source} is measured relative to {target} code structure (e.g., rework as percent of original budget allocation by budget code)
verbGlossary.TRACKED BY: {source} or cost is monitored over time using {target} breakdown structure (e.g., rework cost tracked by WBS code across project lifecycle)
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED TO: {source} is compared against {target} industry threshold or published baseline (e.g., TRIR benchmarked to OSHA industry averages, rework cost benchmarked to AACE norms)
verbGlossary.RATED BY: {source}rate or recurrence rate is scored using {target} grading framework (e.g., hazard recurrence rate rated by OSHA hazard severity, inspection pass rate rated by IBC categories)
verbGlossary.COMPLIES WITH: {source} confirms adherence to {target} (e.g., commissioning observation verifying LEED or WELL criteria)
verbGlossary.REMEDIATES UNDER: {source} or corrective action addresses a finding classified under {target} (e.g., corrective action plan remediating an OSHA fall hazard finding)
verbGlossary.SCOPED BY: {source} task is defined in terms of {target} scope of work (e.g., corrective task scoped to Division 09 finishes specification)
verbGlossary.SEGMENTED BY: {source}is broken down by {target} for comparative analysis (e.g., observation density segmented by MasterFormat division to identify problem trades)
verbGlossary.ESCALATED PER: {source} or deficiency is formally escalated following {target} document's dispute or notice procedures (e.g., NCR escalated per AIA A201 claim provisions)
verbGlossary.ORGANIZED BY: {source} scope is structured according to {target} divisions or sections (e.g., inspection checklist organized by MasterFormat divisions)
